name: Metriport
what: Open-source healthcare interoperability API for patient data exchange, FHIR/C-CDA/PDF normalization, and U.S. health data connectivity.
solves: Connecting to U.S. healthcare records and normalizing patient data from HIEs/providers.
users: Healthcare software teams needing interoperability with U.S. healthcare systems.
fit: Poor fit for Hylono’s current EU wellness stack. Strategic mismatch unless the company enters regulated healthcare interoperability, especially in the U.S.
runtime: Healthcare integration platform with API services, data normalization, and provider/network connectors.
components: API layer, healthcare connectors, data normalization pipelines, and auth/integration management.
deploy: Self-hosting is possible but domain-specific and compliance-heavy.
deps: Healthcare connectivity, auth, secure storage, and domain expertise.
datastores: Healthcare data stores and integration metadata.
auth: API auth and healthcare-integration credential handling.
apis: FHIR/C-CDA/PDF-related APIs and provider connectivity endpoints.
security: Potential PHI and regulated-health data. Very high governance burden.
setup_path: Keep off roadmap unless business strategy changes materially toward regulated healthcare interoperability.
env: Healthcare integration credentials, storage, auth, and service settings.
persistence: Sensitive health data demands strong backup/encryption controls if ever used.
operations: Connector maintenance and compliance would dominate.
failure_points: Confusing health-adjacent wellness operations with healthcare interoperability needs.
verify: Confirm whether any U.S./clinical interoperability requirement exists. It likely does not.
gdpr: Would imply health-data handling and possibly cross-border transfer issues.
overlap: Not meaningfully relevant unless entering healthcare interoperability.
codex_read_first: Read the business-fit and compliance notes; this is likely out of scope.
codex_best_path: Do not implement unless healthcare interoperability becomes an explicit business program.
codex_never_assume: Never equate health-adjacent brand positioning with a need for clinical interoperability software.
